title: LIVE: Federal Reserve Chair Powell holds press conference after FOMC rate decision | NBC News
title_ko: LIVE: 연준 파월 의장, FOMC 금리 결정 이후 기자회견 | NBC 뉴스
transcript: Good afternoon. My colleagues and I remain squarely focused on achieving our dualmandate goals of maximum employment and stable prices for the benefit of the American people. The US economy has been expanding at a solid pace. While job gains have remained low, the unemployment rate has been little changed in recent months and inflation remains somewhat elevated. Today, the FOMC decided to leave our policy rate unchanged. We see the current stance of monetary policy as appropriate to promote progress toward our maximum employment and 2% inflation goals. The implications of developments in the Middle East for the US economy are uncertain. We will remain attentive to risks to both sides of our dual mandate. And I'll have more to say about monetary policy after briefly reviewing economic developments. Available indicators suggest that economic activity has been expanding at a solid pace. Consumer spending has been resilient and business fixed investment has continued to expand. In contrast, activity in the housing sector has remained weak. In our summary of economic projections, the median participant projects that real GDP will rise 2.4% this year and 2.3% next year, somewhat stronger than projected in December. In the labor market, the unemployment rate was 4.4% in February and has changed little since late last summer. Job gains have remained low. A good part of the slowing in the pace of job growth over the past year reflects a decline in the growth of the labor force due to lower immigration and labor force participation. Though labor demand has clearly softened as well. Other indicators, including job openings, layoffs, hiring, and nominal wage growth, generally show little change in recent months. In our SEP, the median projection of the unemployment rate is 4.4% at the end of this year and edges down thereafter. Inflation has eased significantly from its highs in mid 2022, but remainsomewhat elevated relative to our 2% longerrun goal. Estimates based on the cons

minute_summaries.summary: FOMC 금리 3.5~3.75% 동결 발표, 이중 책무 집중 선언
GDP 2.4% 전망, 실업률 4.4% 안정, 소비 탄탄
PCE 2.8% / Core 3.0%, 관세·중동 유가가 주요 원인
SEP 중위 금리전망 3.4% 동결, 금리인하 경로 불확실
통화정책 사전 경로 없음, 데이터 기반 회의별 결정
에너지 인플레이션 무시 여부: 관세 효과 소멸 먼저 확인 필요
Core 3%의 절반이 관세, 에너지 look-through 조심스럽게 접근
SEP 인하 횟수 일부 위원 2회→1회 축소, 중위값은 유지
성장·인플레이션 동시 불확실, 유가 지속 기간 미지수
고용 vs 인플레이션 리스크 균형, 실업률 4.4% 안정적
파월 이사 재직 여부 미결정, 기관 최선 방향으로 결정
1970s 스태그플레이션 아님, 이중 책무 긴장은 있으나 관리 가능
트럼프 유가 급락 주장에 별도 예측 없음으로 답변
관세 인플레이션 소멸 신뢰도 높아져, 연내 중반 개선 기대
법원 판결 후 관세율 하락했으나 행정부 원상복귀 전제 유지
SEP 점도표 구속력 없음, 후임자 제약 없음
소비자 물가 압박 지속, 보험 등 지연 반영 항목 설명
유가→디젤→식품·물류 경로로 Core 인플레이션 파급
유가 충격 규모·지속 기간 불확실, 관망 필요
장기 중립금리 전망 소폭 상향, 미래 정책 여지 축소
기자회견 마무리, Fed의 독립성·객관성·공공 헌신 강조

speech_summary.full_summary: 파월 의장은 2026년 3월 FOMC에서 기준금리를 3.5~3.75%로 동결했다고 발표했다. 미국 경제는 견조한 성장세(GDP 2.4% 전망)를 보이고 있으나 중동 전쟁(Operation Epic Fury)으로 인한 유가 급등이 인플레이션을 밀어올리고 있어 불확실성이 크다. Core PCE 3.0% 중 상당 부분(0.5~0.75%p)은 관세 효과이며, 연내 중반부터 관세 인플레이션 소멸을 기대한다. SEP 기준금리 중위 전망은 12월과 동일하게 유지됐으나 일부 위원들이 인하 횟수를 줄였다. 파월 의장은 스태그플레이션 우려를 일축하고, 트럼프의 유가 급락 주장에 직접 동의하지 않았으며, 의장직 임기 종료 후 이사 재직 여부는 미결정이라고 밝혔다.
key_points: 금리 3.5~3.75% 동결 — 지난 0.75%p 인하로 중립금리 범위 진입, 추가 조정은 데이터 기반
Core PCE 3.0%: 관세 효과 0.5~0.75%p, 중동 유가 상승이 헤드라인·단기 기대 인플레이션 추가 자극
SEP 금리 중위값 3.4%(올해 말) 유지, 일부 위원 인하 횟수 축소(2→1회)로 이견 확대
에너지 인플레이션 look-through 여부: 관세 효과 소멸 확인 후에 검토, 5년 목표 초과 이력 고려
스태그플레이션 아님 — 고용·물가 이중 긴장은 있으나 1970s 수준과 다름
파월 이사 재직 여부 미결정, 점도표 구속력 없어 후임자 정책 경로 자유
speech_summary.market_impact_summary: 금리 동결 자체는 예상 범위였으나 SEP 인하 횟수 축소 신호와 중동 불확실성에 단기채(ZT) 변동성이 가장 컸다. 나스닥은 정책 불확실성 지속에 소폭 하락, 금은 유가·불확실성 헤지 수요로 변동. 파월의 비둘기파·매파 혼재 발언이 자산별 상이한 반응을 유발했다.

top_volatility.transcript_segment: From last September through December, we lowered our policy rate 3/4 of a percentage point, bringing it within a range of plausible estimates of neutral. This normalization of our policy stance should continue to help stabilize the labor market while allowing inflation to resume its downward trend toward 2%. But the implications of events in the Middle East for the US economy are uncertain. In the near term, higher energy prices will push up overall inflation, but it is too soon to know the scope and duration of the potential effects on the economy. We will continue to monitor the risks to both sides of our mandate. We are well positioned to determine the extent and timing of additional adjustments to our policy rate based on the incoming data, thevolving outlook, and the balance of risks. In our SEP, FOMC participants wrote down their individual assessments of an appropriate path of the federal funds rate under what each participant judges to be the most likely scenario for theconomy. The median participant projects that the appropriate level of the federal funds rate will be 3.4%. 4% at the end of this year and 3.1% at the end of next year, unchanged from December. As is always the case, these individual forecasts are subject to uncertainty and they are not a committee plan or decision. Monetary policy is not on a preset course and we'll make our decisions on a meeting bymeating basis. To conclude, the Fed has been assigned two goals for monetary policy. Maximum employment and stable prices. We remain committed to supporting maximum employment, bringing inflation sustainably to our 2% goal, and keeping longerterm inflation expectations well anchored. Our success in delivering on these goals matters to all Americans. We at the Fed will continue to do to do our jobs with objectivity, integrity, and a deep commitment to serve the American people. Thank you, and I look forward to your questions. Colby, thank you. Colby Smith of the New York Times. There's been some debate about whether the Fed should look through the inflation that will come from higher oil prices stemming from the Middle East conflict. Is that the right approach athis juncture? And to what extent does the fact that inflation has been above target for roughly five years now influence the committee's thinking around this? So, uh, first let me say we're well aware of, um, the performance of inflation over the last few years and how a series of shocks have have interrupted progress that we've made over time and that happened most recently with tariffs and then and now there will be some effects on inflation coming forward. Um the the thing that's really important that we see this year is progress uh on inflation through a reduction in goods inflation as the one-time effects on prices of tariffs go through the system, go through theconomy. That's the main thing we're looking for going into this exercise. And we need to be seeing that uh to you know to sort of understand that we actually are making progress because onet we didn't make progress. And if you look at total inflation, sorry, total core inflation, it's about 3%. And some big chunk of that between a half and 3/4 is actually tariffs. So we're looking for progress on that. The question of whether we look through uh the energy inflation doesn't really arise until we have kind of checked that box. It of course is kind of standard learning that you look through energy shocks, buthat's always been dependent on on uh inflation expectations remaining well anchored. And I think now it's also dependent on on what you mentioned which is that broader context of of 5 years now of inflation above target. We have to keep all of those things in mind and the question of looking through when it does arise will be one to approach not lightly but you know in in the context
top_volatility.transcript_segment_ko: 파월 의장은 금리를 3.5~3.75%로 동결했다고 발표하며 기자회견 시작 발표를 마쳤다. 작년 9~12월 기준금리를 0.75%p 인하해 중립 범위에 근접했으며, 이 기조는 노동시장 안정과 2% 물가 목표 달성을 지원할 것이라고 밝혔다. 중동 사태(이란 전쟁)로 인한 미국 경제 영향은 불확실하며 단기적으로 에너지 가격 상승이 인플레이션을 밀어올릴 것이나 그 범위와 지속 기간은 아직 알 수 없다고 했다. SEP 기준 올해 말 기준금리 중위값은 3.4~4%, 내년 말 3.1%로 12월과 동일하며, 통화정책은 사전 설정 경로가 아닌 데이터 기반으로 회의마다 결정한다고 강조했다. 핵심 인플레이션(Core PCE) 약 3% 중 0.5~0.75%는 관세 효과이며, 에너지 인플레이션을 '일시적으로 무시(look through)'하는 문제는 관세 효과 소멸을 먼저 확인한 뒤에 검토할 사안이라고 설명했다.
연설 종료 후 시장 반응 구간

This normalization of our policy stance should continue to help stabilize the labor market while allowing inflation to resume its downward trend toward 2%. We will continue to monitor the risks to both sides of our mandate. In our SEP, FOMC participants wrote down their individual assessments of an appropriate path of the federal funds rate under what each participant judges to be the most likely scenario for theconomy. The median participant projects that the appropriate level of the federal funds rate will be 3.4%. 4% at the end of this year and 3.1% at the end of next year, unchanged from December. As is always the case, these individual forecasts are subject to uncertainty and they are not a committee plan or decision. Monetary policy is not on a preset course and we'll make our decisions on a meeting bymeating basis. To conclude, the Fed has been assigned two goals for monetary policy. Maximum employment and stable prices. We remain committed to supporting maximum employment, bringing inflation sustainably to our 2% goal, and keeping longerterm inflation expectations well anchored. Our success in delivering on these goals matters to all Americans. We at the Fed will continue to do to do our jobs with objectivity, integrity, and a deep commitment to serve the American people. Thank you, and I look forward to your questions. Colby, thank you. Colby Smith of the New York Times. There's been some debate about whether the Fed should look through the inflation that will come from higher oil prices stemming from the Middle East conflict. Is that the right approach athis juncture? And to what extent does the fact that inflation has been above target for roughly five years now influence the committee's thinking around this? So, uh, first let me say we're well aware of, um, the performance of inflation over the last few years and how a series of shocks have have interrupted progress that we've made over time and that happened most recently with tariffs and then and now there will be some effects on inflation coming forward. Um the the thing that's really important that we see this year is progress uh on inflation through a reduction in goods inflation as the one-time effects on prices of tariffs go

through the economy. And so what we've been doing is what our staff's been doing, uh, it's very interesting. They, you know, they started off with just an estimate because there wasn't a real history and then they've built based on the history that they're seeing of tariffs coming through into prices. Um, they've now they can now have an arc and for all the tariffs they can say, well, you know, so we have, I think, a slightly more confidence that we will see tariff inflation coming down. Not prices, but you won't see further increases. and we should start seeing that uh more and more in the middle parts of the year. We do expect that. Um now, you're also right though that the level of um of tariffs came down fairly meaningfully in the wake of the uh uh in the wake of the court decision, but but the administration said they're going to they're going to move they're going to get that rate right back up to where it was. So, and we we you know, we we assume that they'll do that over time. So, um that's how we think about it. Thank you, Chris. Hi. Uh, thank you. Uh, Chris Rugver at Associated Press. I wanted, you talked about the shortcomings of the SCP in this environment. I wanted to ask about or throw in an additional thing, which is the fact that it's happening before transition. Is there still some value perhaps in the public knowing what other Fed officials are thinking, uh, the ones who will stay on for the rest of the year and beyond? And does that even handicap your successor in any way? Well, just in terms of having a whole committee expressing its views, is that something that locks them in for the rest of the year? No, no, no. We never The SCP is never uh people are more than happy to change their their SCP dots. They're no way bound by them. you know, it's it's it's your opinion at a moment in time which can change based on events, you know, sometimes very quickly. Um, so it doesn't it never locks people in. You know, people are more than happy to be proven wrong in either direction. And uh, so I I I just think we do it and you know, we this was I mentioned this was a time when it's very hard to do it. During the pandemic, we actually took one meeting off I think and but you know, we don't we don't like to do that just because it's hard to do. we should just keep doing it. And but I just would say for this one though, I think it's more than even more than usual good to take it take the forecast with a grain of salt because subject to just very high levels of uncertainty Brian. Hi Chel Brian Chung with NBC News. Uh so you mentioned at the beginning of this conversation that you're well aware of the history of inflation running above target. It seems like that might be what's underpinning the affordability problem that we're hearing a lot about uh on Main Street. Are there any signs to you that that's impacting the psychology of the consumer, especially given that they're a big driver of overall economic activity? I you know I'm not sure what's what that's all about but what I'll tell you what I think and what we see in surveys and that is you know people there were big price increases um all around the world by the way everywhere in in countries like the United States had basically the same kind of thing. I think it was a global inflation coming out of the pandemic and everywhere people are feeling like theyou know real wages have been going up in the United States now for 3 years roughly. Um but people are not feeling like they're not feeling good about it yet and it will take some years of positive real real earning gains for people to feel good again we think. But you're right, we uh but when you talk to people, they're they they do feel squeezed and you know there there areas where where uh prices are still going up like insurance, various different kinds of insurance are getting more more and more expensive and that's just catching up really from inflationary pressures that take a while to get to the price into the price. So we take it very seriously. We don't
top_volatility.transcript_segment_ko: 관세 인플레이션 경로에 대한 연준 스태프의 신뢰도가 높아졌으며, 연내 중반부터 관세 인플레이션이 소멸하기 시작할 것으로 예상한다고 밝혔다. 법원 판결로 관세율이 낮아졌으나 행정부가 원래 수준으로 복귀한다는 전제하에 시나리오를 구성한다고 했다. 파월 의장 퇴임 전 마지막 SEP라는 점에서 후임자 제약 여부 질문에 대해 SEP 점도표는 구속력이 없으며 언제든 바꿀 수 있다고 답했다. 소비자들이 3년간 실질 임금 상승에도 불구하고 물가 압박을 느끼는 이유는 보험 등 지연 반영 항목 때문이며, 완전히 해소되려면 수년이 걸릴 것이라고 설명했다.

top_volatility.transcript_segment_ko: 관세 인플레이션 소멸을 확인해야 에너지 인플레이션 무시 여부를 논의할 수 있다고 설명했다. SEP 중위값은 변화 없지만 일부 위원들이 금리인하 횟수를 2회에서 1회로 줄였으며, 올해 인플레이션 개선을 전제로 인하 여지를 열어두고 있다고 밝혔다.

top_volatility.transcript_segment: policy as appropriate to promote progress toward our maximum employment and 2% inflation goals. The implications of developments in the Middle East for the US economy are uncertain. We will remain attentive to risks to both sides of our dual mandate. And I'll have more to say about monetary policy after briefly reviewing economic developments. Available indicators suggest that economic activity has been expanding at a solid pace. Consumer spending has been resilient and business fixed investment has continued to expand. In contrast, activity in the housing sector has remained weak. In our summary of economic projections, the median participant projects that real GDP will rise 2.4% this year and 2.3% next year, somewhat stronger than projected in December. In the labor market, the unemployment rate was 4.4% in February and has changed little since late last summer. Job gains have remained low. A good part of the slowing in the pace of job growth over the past year reflects a decline in the growth of the labor force due to lower immigration and labor force participation. Though labor demand has clearly softened as well. Other indicators, including job openings, layoffs, hiring, and nominal wage growth, generally show little change in recent months. In our SEP, the median projection of the unemployment rate is 4.4% at the end of this year and edges down thereafter. Inflation has eased significantly from its highs in mid 2022, but remainsomewhat elevated relative to our 2% longerrun goal. Estimates based on the consumer price index and other data indicate that total PCE prices rose 2.8% over the 12 months ending in February and that excluding the volatile food and energy categories core PCE prices rose 3.0%. These elevated readings largely reflect inflation in the goods sector which has been boosted by the effects of tariffs. Near-term measures of inflation expectations have risen in recent weeks, likely reflecting the substantial rise in oil prices caused by the supply disruptions in the Middle East. Most measures of longerterm expectations remain consistent with our 2% inflation goal. The median projection in the SEP for total PCE inflation this year is 2.7% and 2.2% next year, a bit higher than projected in December. Our monetary policy actions are guided by our dual mandate to promote maximum employment and stable prices for the American people. At today's meeting, the committee decided to maintain the target range for the federal funds rate at 3 and 1 half to 3 and 3/4%.
top_volatility.transcript_segment_ko: 파월 의장의 기자회견 오프닝 발표. 미국 경제는 견조한 속도로 확장 중이며 소비 지출은 탄탄하고 기업 투자는 꾸준하나 주택 부문은 부진하다고 평가했다. SEP 중위 전망: GDP 2.4%(올해)·2.3%(내년), 실업률 4.4% 유지, 총 PCE 인플레이션 2.7%(올해)·2.2%(내년). 중동 사태로 인한 유가 급등이 근원물가 기대를 끌어올리고 있으나 장기 기대치는 2% 목표와 일치한다고 밝혔다. 이중 책무(최대 고용·물가 안정) 달성에 집중하며 금리는 3.5~3.75%로 동결한다고 발표했다.

Uh, that's what we've done on several occasions, including involving me, and it's what we're going to do in this situation.
